- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

81	    """Return True if this error should count against the circuit breaker.

83	    Permanent errors (invalid args, not found, etc.) should NOT trip the breaker
84	    because they indicate a bug or misconfiguration, not a service outage.
85	    """
86	    if isinstance(exc, aio.AioRpcError):
87	        return exc.code() not in _PERMANENT_CODES
88	    return True

91	class GrpcClient:
92	    """Async gRPC client wrapping the generated AgentNervousSystemStub.
93	
94	    Features:
95	    - Exponential backoff retry on transient errors (UNAVAILABLE, etc.)
96	    - Circuit breaker: opens after 5 consecutive failures, 30s recovery
97	    - Permanent errors skip retry and don't trip the breaker
98	    """
99	
100	    def __init__(self, host: str | None = None, port: int | None = None) -> None:
101	        config = get_config()
102	        self._host = host or config.grpc_host
103	        self._port = port or config.grpc_port
104	        self._target = f"{self._host}:{self._port}"
105	        self._channel: aio.Channel | None = None
106	        self._stub: AgentNervousSystemStub | None = None
107	        self._breaker = CircuitBreaker(name=f"grpc:{self._target}")
108	
109	    @property
110	    def target(self) -> str:
111	        return self._target
112	
113	    @property
114	    def connected(self) -> bool:
115	        return self._channel is not None
116	
117	    @property
118	    def breaker(self) -> CircuitBreaker:
119	        return self._breaker
120	
121	    async def connect(self) -> None:
122	        """Open the gRPC channel to the Rust daemon."""
123	        if self._channel is not None:
124	            return
125	        self._channel = aio.insecure_channel(self._target)
126	        self._stub = AgentNervousSystemStub(self._channel)
127	        logger.info("grpc_client: connected to %s", self._target)
128	
129	    async def close(self) -> None:
130	        """Close the gRPC channel."""
131	        if self._channel is not None:
132	            await self._channel.close()
133	            self._channel = None
134	            self._stub = None
135	            logger.info("grpc_client: disconnected")
136	
137	    async def _ensure_connected(self) -> AgentNervousSystemStub:
138	        if self._stub is None:
139	            await self.connect()
140	        assert self._stub is not None
141	        return self._stub
142	
143	    async def _call(
144	        self,
145	        coro_factory: Callable[[], Awaitable[Any]],
146	        method_name: str,
147	    ) -> Any:
148	        """Wrap a gRPC call with circuit breaker and exponential backoff retry.
149	
150	        Args:
151	            coro_factory: An async callable that returns the gRPC result.
152	            method_name: Human-readable method name for log messages.
153	        """
154	        if self._breaker.is_open:
155	            raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(
156	                f"Circuit breaker is OPEN for {self._target}. "
157	                f"Method '{method_name}' rejected."
158	            )
159	
160	        @retry(
161	            stop=stop_after_attempt(3),
162	            wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=0.5, max=10),
163	            retry=retry_if_exception(_is_retryable),
164	            reraise=True,
165	        )
166	        async def _with_retry() -> Any:
167	            return await coro_factory()
168	
169	        try:
170	            result = await _with_retry()
171	        except RetryError as exc:
172	            # Retries exhausted — the wrapped exception is in exc.__cause__
173	            cause = exc.__cause__ or exc
174	            if _should_trip_breaker(cause):
175	                self._breaker.record_failure()
176	            logger.error(
177	                "grpc_client: %s failed after retries: %s", method_name, cause
178	            )
179	            raise cause from None
180	        except Exception as exc:
181	            if _should_trip_breaker(exc):
182	                self._breaker.record_failure()
183	            raise
184	        else:
185	            self._breaker.record_success()
186	            return result
187	
188	    @staticmethod
189	    def _pb_to_dict(pb_msg: Any) -> Any:
190	        """Convert a protobuf message to a plain dict, keeping scalars as-is."""
191	        if pb_msg is None:
192	            return None
193	        try:
194	            return MessageToDict(pb_msg, preserving_proto_field_name=True)
195	        except Exception:
196	            return pb_msg

1	"""Agent Execution Loop — full perception→decision→action→learning cycle.
2	
3	Orchestrates:
4	1. Decompose goal → sub_goals
5	2. For each sub_goal:
6	   a. Perceive (all 5 Eyes)
7	   b. Synthesize (CrossEyeCoordinator)
8	   c. Decide (AgentPlanner — cold or warm start)
9	   d. Execute (gRPC client)
10	   e. Learn (DecisionIntelligence — score → embed → store)
11	   f. Verify (GoalVerifier)
12	3. Re-plan on failure, escalate on deadlock
13	"""
14

18	import time

65	    """Final result of an agent loop run."""
66	
67	    success: bool = False
68	    total_steps: int = 0
69	    sub_goals_completed: int = 0
70	    total_sub_goals: int = 0

78	class AgentLoop:
79	    """Main execution loop tying together all components.
80	
81	    Usage:
82	        loop = AgentLoop(session_id="s1")
83	
84	        result = await loop.run(
85	            goal="Search for flights from Delhi to Mumbai on June 5",
86	            context={"budget_cents": 500},
87	        )
88	    """
89	
90	    def __init__(
91	        self,
92	        session_id: str,
93	        *,
94	        grpc: GrpcClient | None = None,
95	        decomposer: GoalDecomposer | None = None,
96	        coordinator: CrossEyeCoordinator | None = None,
97	        planner: AgentPlanner | None = None,
98	        intelligence: DecisionIntelligence | None = None,
99	        max_steps: int = _DEFAULT_MAX_STEPS,
100	        max_errors_per_subgoal: int = _DEFAULT_MAX_ERRORS_PER_SUBGOAL,
101	    ) -> None:
102	        self.session_id = session_id
103	        self._browser_session_id: str = ""  # daemon-issued UUID, set in run()
104	        self._grpc = grpc or get_grpc_client()
105	        self._decomposer = decomposer or GoalDecomposer()
106	        self._coordinator = coordinator or CrossEyeCoordinator()
107	        self._intelligence = intelligence or DecisionIntelligence()
108	        self._planner = planner or AgentPlanner(intelligence=self._intelligence)
109	        self._max_steps = max_steps
110	        self._max_errors_per_subgoal = max_errors_per_subgoal
111	
112	        # Load runtime config for fast-mode toggles
113	        self._runtime = get_config().runtime
114	        logger.debug(
115	            "agent_loop: mode=%s fast_coord=%s skip_vision=%s skip_diff=%s",
116	            self._runtime.mode,
117	            self._runtime.fast_coordinator,
118	            self._runtime.skip_vision_in_fast,
119	            self._runtime.skip_diff_in_fast,
120	        )
121	
122	        # Eyes (created once, reused)
123	        self._eyes = {
124	            "dom_reader": DomReaderEye(),
125	            "vision": VisionEye(),
126	            "page_diff": PageDiffEye(),
127	            "goal_verifier": GoalVerifierEye(),
128	            "error_detector": ErrorDetectorEye(),
129	        }
130	
131	    # ── Public API ─────────────────────────────────────────────
132	
133	    async def run(
134	        self,
135	        goal: str,
136	        context: dict | None = None,
137	        *,
138	        daemon_goal_id: str | None = None,
139	    ) -> LoopResult:
140	        """Execute the full agent loop for a goal.
141	
142	        If daemon_goal_id is provided, uses the daemon's UUID instead
143	        of generating a hash-based ID.
144	        """
145	        state = LoopState()
146	        t_start = time.monotonic()
147	
148	        logger.info("agent_loop: starting goal='%s' session=%s", goal[:80], self.session_id)
149	
150	        effective_goal_id = daemon_goal_id or str(_uuid.uuid4())
151	        result = LoopResult(success=False, total_sub_goals=0)
152	
153	        try:
154	            # 0. Create browser session
155	            session = await self._grpc.create_session(
156	                goal_id=effective_goal_id,
157	            )
158	            self._browser_session_id = session.session_id
159	            logger.info("agent_loop: browser session=%s", self._browser_session_id)
160	
161	            # 1-6. Run full pipeline
162	            result = await self._run_impl(goal, context, state, t_start, effective_goal_id)
163	
164	        except Exception as exc:
165	            logger.warning("agent_loop: failed: %s", exc)
166	            result = LoopResult(
167	                success=False,
168	                summary=f"Failed: {exc}",
169	                total_sub_goals=0,
170	            )
171	
172	        finally:
173	            # Always report to daemon
174	            if daemon_goal_id:
175	                try:
176	                    status = "completed" if result.success else "failed"
177	                    await self._grpc.update_goal_progress(
178	                        goal_id=daemon_goal_id,
179	                        progress=1.0 if result.success else 0.0,
180	                        status=status,
181	                    )
182	                except Exception:
183	                    pass
184	
185	        return result
186	
187	    async def close(self) -> None:
188	        """Close the browser session. Call when done with the AgentLoop."""
189	        if self._browser_session_id:
190	            try:
191	                await self._grpc.close_session(self._browser_session_id, reason="loop done")
192	            except Exception:
193	                pass
194	
195	    async def _run_impl(
196	        self,
197	        goal: str,
198	        context: dict | None,
199	        state: LoopState,
200	        t_start: float,
201	        goal_id: str,
202	    ) -> LoopResult:
203	        # 1. Decompose goal
204	        state.goal_spec = await self._decomposer.decompose(goal, context)
205	        if not state.goal_spec.sub_goals:
206	            logger.warning("agent_loop: no sub-goals produced")
207	            return LoopResult(
208	                success=False,
209	                summary="Goal decomposition produced no sub-goals.",
210	                total_sub_goals=0,
211	            )
212	
213	        # Cap sub-goals to prevent over-decomposition
214	        state.goal_spec.sub_goals = state.goal_spec.sub_goals[:5]
215	        sg_count = len(state.goal_spec.sub_goals)
216	
217	        # Push LLM-decomposed sub-goals back to daemon
218	        if goal_id:
219	            try:
220	                sub_goals_dict = [
221	                    {
222	                        "id": sg.id, "description": sg.description,
223	                        "success_criteria": sg.success_criteria,
224	                        "depends_on": sg.depends_on, "status": sg.status,
225	                    }
226	                    for sg in state.goal_spec.sub_goals
227	                ]
228	                await self._grpc.update_goal_progress(
229	                    goal_id=goal_id, progress=0.0, status="active",
230	                    sub_goals=sub_goals_dict,
231	                )
232	                logger.info("agent_loop: pushed %d LLM sub-goals to daemon", sg_count)
233	            except Exception:
234	                pass
235	
236	        # 2. Iterate sub-goals
237	        for i, sub_goal in enumerate(state.goal_spec.sub_goals):
238	            if state.done:
239	                break
240	
241	            state.current_sub_goal_index = i
242	            logger.info(
243	                "agent_loop: sub_goal %d/%d: '%s'",
244	                i + 1, sg_count, sub_goal.description[:80],
245	            )
246	
247	            sub_result = await self._execute_sub_goal(
248	                goal=goal,
249	                goal_id=goal_id,
250	                sub_goal=sub_goal,
251	                state=state,
252	                context=context or {},
253	            )
254	
255	            # Report incremental progress to daemon
256	            if goal_id:
257	                try:
258	                    progress = (i + 1) / sg_count if sg_count > 0 else 0.0
259	                    await self._grpc.update_goal_progress(
260	                        goal_id=goal_id,
261	                        progress=min(progress, 0.99),
262	                        status="active",
263	                    )
264	                except Exception:
265	                    pass
266	
267	            if sub_result.get("escalate"):
268	                return LoopResult(
269	                    success=False,
270	                    total_steps=state.step_count,
271	                    sub_goals_completed=i,
272	                    total_sub_goals=sg_count,
273	                    total_errors=state.total_errors,
274	                    summary=f"Escalated at sub-goal {i+1}/{sg_count}: {sub_result.get('reason', '')}",
275	                    alerts=state.goal_spec.risk_factors or [],
276	                )
277	
278	        # 3. Build result
279	        elapsed = time.monotonic() - t_start
280	        completed = state.current_sub_goal_index + (1 if not state.done else 0)
281	
282	        logger.info(
283	            "agent_loop: done steps=%d completed=%d/%d errors=%d elapsed=%.1fs",
284	            state.step_count, min(completed, sg_count), sg_count,
285	            state.total_errors, elapsed,
286	        )
287	
288	        return LoopResult(
289	            success=not state.done or completed >= sg_count,
290	            total_steps=state.step_count,
291	            sub_goals_completed=min(completed, sg_count),
292	            total_sub_goals=sg_count,
293	            total_errors=state.total_errors,
294	            summary=state.final_summary or f"Completed {min(completed, sg_count)}/{sg_count} sub-goals in {state.step_count} steps.",
295	        )
296	
297	    # ── Sub-goal execution ────────────────────────────────────
298	
299	    async def _execute_sub_goal(
300	        self,
301	        goal: str,
302	        goal_id: str,
303	        sub_goal: SubGoal,
304	        state: LoopState,
305	        context: dict,
306	    ) -> dict:
307	        """Execute a single sub-goal: perceive → decide → act → learn → verify."""
308	        errors_this_subgoal = 0
309	        sub_goal_done = False
310	
311	        while not sub_goal_done and state.step_count < self._max_steps:
312	            state.step_count += 1
313	
314	            # ── Perceive ──────────────────────────────────────
315	            page_data = await self._gather_page_data(state)
316	
317	            # DOM Reader runs first (deterministic, no LLM)
318	            eye_reports: list[EyeReport] = []
319	            dom_report = await self._eyes["dom_reader"].observe(
320	                self.session_id, page_data,
321	            )
322	            eye_reports.append(dom_report)
323	
324	            # Vision + Error Detector run in parallel (LLM-bound)
325	            # PageDiff LLM eliminated: structural diff feeds coordinator fallback.
326	            # Vision gating: fast+skip → never; thorough+clean → every 3rd cycle
327	            dom_content = dom_report.content if isinstance(dom_report.content, dict) else {}
328	            has_overlays = bool(dom_content.get("distraction_flags"))
329	            is_fast = self._runtime.mode == "fast"
330	
331	            parallel_tasks = []
332	            _run_vision = page_data.get("screenshot_base64") is not None
333	            if _run_vision and is_fast and self._runtime.skip_vision_in_fast:
334	                _run_vision = False
335	            elif _run_vision and not has_overlays:
336	                _run_vision = state.step_count % 3 == 0
337	            if _run_vision:
338	                parallel_tasks.append(self._eyes["vision"].observe(self.session_id, page_data))
339	
340	            if state.last_error:
341	                parallel_tasks.append(self._eyes["error_detector"].observe(
342	                    self.session_id, {
343	                        "action_description": (
344	                            state.action_history[-1] if state.action_history else "unknown"
345	                        ),
346	                        "error_message": state.last_error,
347	                        "page_url": state.last_page_url,
348	                        "page_title": state.last_page_title,
349	                        "goal_context": goal,
350	                    },
351	                ))
352	            if parallel_tasks:
353	                parallel_results = await asyncio.gather(*parallel_tasks, return_exceptions=True)
354	                for r in parallel_results:
355	                    if isinstance(r, Exception):
356	                        logger.warning("Eye failed in parallel: %s", r)
357	                    else:
358	                        eye_reports.append(r)
359	
360	            # ── Synthesize ────────────────────────────────────
361	            # Deterministic coordinator (always — zero LLM cost).
362	            # Structural diff from Rust engine feeds fallback directly.
363	            diff_data = state.last_diff or {}
364	            signal = self._coordinator._fallback_synthesize(eye_reports, diff_data=diff_data)
365	
366	            # ── Decide ────────────────────────────────────────
367	            dom_elements = self._extract_interactive_elements(page_data)
368	            page_type = self._extract_page_type(signal)
369	
370	            planned = await self._planner.plan_next_action(
371	                goal_context=goal,
372	                sub_goal=sub_goal.description,
373	                sub_goal_criteria=sub_goal.success_criteria,
374	                unified_perception=signal.unified_perception,
375	                available_elements=dom_elements,
376	                action_history=state.action_history,
377	                last_error=state.last_error,
378	                page_type=page_type,
379	            )
380	
381	            logger.info(
382	                "agent_loop: step %d action=%s selector=%s source=%s conf=%.2f",
383	                state.step_count, planned.action_type, planned.selector,
384	                planned.source, planned.confidence,
385	            )
386	
387	            if planned.action_type == "escalate":
388	                return {"escalate": True, "reason": planned.reasoning}
389	
390	            # ── Execute (skip for terminal "done") ───────────
391	            if planned.action_type != "done":
392	                t_action = time.monotonic()
393	                try:
394	                    action_result = await self._execute_action(planned, dom_elements)
395	                    execution_time_ms = int((time.monotonic() - t_action) * 1000)
396	                    action_succeeded = True
397	                    result_text = action_result.get("message", "")
398	                    exec_error = None
399	                except Exception as exc:
400	                    execution_time_ms = int((time.monotonic() - t_action) * 1000)
401	                    action_succeeded = False
402	                    result_text = ""
403	                    exec_error = str(exc)
404	                    state.last_error = exec_error
405	                    errors_this_subgoal += 1
406	                    state.total_errors += 1
407	
408	                    logger.warning(
409	                        "agent_loop: action failed step=%d error=%s",
410	                        state.step_count, exec_error[:120],
411	                    )
412	            else:
413	                execution_time_ms = 0
414	                action_succeeded = True
415	                result_text = ""
416	                exec_error = None
417	
418	            state.action_history.append(
419	                f"{planned.action_type} {planned.selector} {planned.value}".strip()
420	            )
421	
422	            # ── Learn ─────────────────────────────────────────
423	            record = ActionRecord(
424	                session_id=self.session_id,
425	                goal_id=goal_id,
426	                action_type=planned.action_type,
427	                selector=planned.selector,
428	                value=planned.value,
429	                tool=planned.tool,
430	                context_type=page_type,
431	                goal_description=goal,
432	                page_type=page_type,
433	                action_succeeded=action_succeeded,
434	                results_produced=result_text,
435	                error_message=exec_error,
436	                goal_advanced=action_succeeded and not exec_error,
437	                criterion_met=None,
438	                sub_goal_completed=False,
439	                execution_time_ms=execution_time_ms,
440	            )
441	            await self._intelligence.record_action(record)
442	
443	            # ── Verify sub-goal ───────────────────────────────
444	            # Boundary-only verification: run at sub-goal completion ("done")
445	            # or when errors accumulate. Eliminates ~80% of per-step verifier
446	            # calls with zero accuracy loss — the Planner already detects
447	            # completion and error conditions at step level.
448	            _should_verify = (
449	                errors_this_subgoal >= 2
450	                or planned.action_type == "done"
451	            )
452	            if _should_verify:
453	                verifier_report = await self._eyes["goal_verifier"].observe(
454	                    self.session_id, {
455	                        "sub_goal_description": sub_goal.description,
456	                        "success_criteria": sub_goal.success_criteria,
457	                        "page_url": state.last_page_url,
458	                        "page_title": state.last_page_title,
459	                        "visible_text": page_data.get("visible_text", []),
460	                        "dom_summary": json_safe(page_data.get("distilled_dom", {})),
461	                    },
462	                )
463	
464	                if verifier_report.content.get("criteria_met") and verifier_report.confidence > 0.6:
465	                    sub_goal_done = True
466	                    state.final_summary = (
467	                        f"Sub-goal '{sub_goal.description[:60]}' verified: "
468	                        f"{verifier_report.content.get('reasoning', '')[:200]}"
469	                    )
470	
471	            # Reset error state on success
472	            if action_succeeded:
473	                state.last_error = ""
474	
475	            # Error gating
476	            if errors_this_subgoal >= self._max_errors_per_subgoal:
477	                return {
478	                    "escalate": True,
479	                    "reason": (
480	                        f"Sub-goal '{sub_goal.description[:80]}' failed {errors_this_subgoal}x "
481	                        f"(max {self._max_errors_per_subgoal}). Last error: {state.last_error[:200]}"
482	                    ),
483	                }
484	
485	        return {"escalate": False, "done": sub_goal_done}
486	
487	    # ── Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────
488	
489	    async def _gather_page_data(self, state: LoopState) -> dict[str, Any]:
490	        """Gather current page state from gRPC daemon."""
491	        page_data: dict[str, Any] = {}
492	
493	        try:
494	            dom = await self._grpc.get_distilled_dom(self._browser_session_id, mode="all_fields")
495	            state.last_distilled_dom = dom
496	            page_data["distilled_dom"] = dom
497	            if dom:
498	                state.last_page_url = dom.get("url", state.last_page_url)
499	                state.last_page_title = dom.get("title", state.last_page_title)
500	        except Exception:
501	            pass
502	
503	        try:
504	            screenshot = await self._grpc.capture_screenshot(self._browser_session_id)
505	            state.last_screenshot_b64 = screenshot.get("data", "")
506	            page_data["screenshot_base64"] = state.last_screenshot_b64
507	        except Exception:
508	            pass
509	
510	        try:
511	            if state.previous_distilled_dom and state.last_distilled_dom:
512	                diff = await self._grpc.compute_diff(
513	                    self._browser_session_id,
514	                    before=state.previous_distilled_dom,
515	                    after=state.last_distilled_dom,
516	                )
517	                state.last_diff = diff
518	                page_data["diff"] = diff
519	        except Exception:
520	            pass
521	        state.previous_distilled_dom = state.last_distilled_dom
522	
523	        page_data["page_url"] = state.last_page_url
524	        page_data["page_title"] = state.last_page_title
525	        page_data["goal_context"] = ""
526	
527	        return page_data
528	
529	    async def _execute_action(self, planned: PlannedAction, dom_elements: list[dict] | None = None) -> dict:
530	        """Execute a planned action via gRPC with parameter validation."""
531	        sid = self._browser_session_id
532	        action_type = planned.action_type
533	
534	        # Validate navigate has URL
535	        if action_type == "navigate":
536	            if not planned.value:
537	                raise ValueError("Navigate action requires a non-empty URL")
538	            return await self._grpc.navigate(sid, planned.value)
539	
540	        # Validate selector exists in DOM for click/fill/type/select
541	        if action_type in ("click", "fill", "type", "select", "submit"):
542	            if not planned.selector:
543	                raise ValueError(f"Action '{action_type}' requires a selector")
544	
545	            # Fallback: if selector not in DOM, use top-scored visible element
546	            if dom_elements and planned.selector not in {e.get("selector") for e in dom_elements}:
547	                logger.warning("agent_loop: selector '%s' not in DOM, using fallback", planned.selector)
548	                if dom_elements:
549	                    planned.selector = dom_elements[0].get("selector", "")
550	                    planned.confidence *= 0.5
551	
552	        # Map 'fill' → 'type' — the Rust daemon supports 'type' for text input
553	        daemon_action = "type" if action_type == "fill" else action_type
554	
555	        return await self._grpc.execute_action(
556	            sid,
557	            action_type=daemon_action,
558	            selector=planned.selector,
559	            value=planned.value,
560	        )
561	
562	    @staticmethod
563	    def _extract_interactive_elements(page_data: dict) -> list[dict]:
564	        """Extract interactive elements from DOM data for the planner.
565	
566	        Merges ``elements`` (distilled DOM) and ``interactive`` (CDP-inspected)
567	        to give the Planner real CSS selectors it can use reliably.
568	        Caps at 30 elements sorted by goal relevance, text truncated to 60 chars.
569	        """
570	        dom = page_data.get("distilled_dom", {})
571	        if isinstance(dom, str):
572	            return []
573	        seen = set()
574	        result = []
575	        for source in (dom.get("interactive", []), dom.get("elements", [])):
576	            for e in (source or []):
577	                sel = e.get("selector", "")
578	                if not sel or sel in seen:
579	                    continue
580	                seen.add(sel)
581	                if e.get("is_visible", True) and e.get("is_enabled", True):
582	                    text = e.get("text", "")
583	                    if len(text) > 60:
584	                        text = text[:57] + "..."
585	                    result.append({
586	                        "selector": sel,
587	                        "tag": e.get("tag", ""),
588	                        "text": text,
589	                        "aria_role": e.get("aria_role", ""),
590	                        "is_visible": True,
591	                        "is_enabled": True,
592	                    })
593	        # Sort by goal_relevance_score descending, take top 30
594	        _dom = dom
595	        relevance_map: dict[str, float] = {}
596	        for source in (_dom.get("interactive", []), _dom.get("elements", [])):
597	            for e in (source or []):
598	                sel = e.get("selector", "")
599	                if sel and "goal_relevance_score" in e:
600	                    try:
601	                        relevance_map[sel] = float(e["goal_relevance_score"])
602	                    except (TypeError, ValueError):
603	                        pass
604	        result.sort(
605	            key=lambda e: relevance_map.get(e["selector"], 0),
606	            reverse=True,
607	        )
608	        return result[:30]
609	
610	    @staticmethod
611	    def _extract_page_type(signal: RoutedSignal) -> str:
612	        """Extract page type from the coordinator's unified perception."""
613	        perception = signal.unified_perception.lower()
614	        for pt in ["search_form", "search_results", "product_page", "checkout",
615	                    "login", "error", "article", "dashboard", "captcha", "paywall"]:
616	            if pt in perception:
617	                return pt
618	        return "unknown"
619	
620	    @staticmethod
621	    def _sanitize_goal_id(goal: str) -> str:
622	        """Create a short stable goal ID from the goal text."""
623	        import hashlib
624	        return hashlib.sha256(goal.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]
625

627	def json_safe(obj: Any) -> str:
628	    """Convert an object to a safe JSON string for prompt use."""
629	    import json as _json
630	    try:
631	        return _json.dumps(obj, default=str)
632	    except Exception:
633	        return str(obj)[:1000]

1	"""Agent Planner — dual-mode action planning.
2	
3	Cold start (no memory): LLM reasons purely from context — goal, page state,
4	action history, and what went wrong. "What happened, why, and what next."
5	
6	Warm start (has memory): Query LanceDB for top-k scored actions from similar
7	contexts, then use LLM to validate/override the recommendation against
8	current page reality.
9	"""
10	
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	import json
14	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
15	from typing import Any
16	
17	from ans_nerves.config import get_config
18	from ans_nerves.llm.client import get_llm_client
19	from ans_nerves.llm.prompts import (
20	    PLANNER_JSON_SCHEMA,
21	    PLANNER_SYSTEM,
22	    build_planner_user_prompt,
23	)
24	from ans_nerves.logging import get_logger
25	from ans_nerves.scoring.embeddings import get_embedding_generator
26	from ans_nerves.scoring.intelligence import DecisionIntelligence, ScoredAction
27	
28	logger = get_logger(__name__)
29	
30	# Minimum records before switching to warm-start mode
31	_WARM_START_MIN_RECORDS = 3
32	# Minimum composite score for a memory recommendation to be used
33	_MIN_RECOMMENDATION_SCORE = 0.3
34	
35	
36	@dataclass
37	class PlannedAction:
38	    """A single planned browser action with reasoning."""
39	
40	    action_type: str = ""
41	    selector: str = ""
42	    value: str = ""
43	    tool: str = ""
44	    reasoning: str = ""
45	    confidence: float = 0.5
46	    expected_outcome: str = ""
47	    source: str = "llm_cold"  # "llm_cold", "memory_validated", "memory_override", "fallback"
48	
49	
50	class AgentPlanner:
51	    """Decides the next browser action using dual-mode reasoning.
52	
53	    Cold start: No scored outcomes in LanceDB → LLM reasons from context.
54	    Warm start: 3+ scored outcomes → query memory, LLM validates recommendation.
55	    """
56	
57	    def __init__(
58	        self,
59	        intelligence: DecisionIntelligence | None = None,
60	    ) -> None:
61	        self._intelligence = intelligence or DecisionIntelligence()
62	
63	    @property
64	    def total_records(self) -> int:
65	        return self._intelligence.total_records
66	
67	    # ── Public API ─────────────────────────────────────────────
68	
69	    async def plan_next_action(
70	        self,
71	        goal_context: str,
72	        *,
73	        sub_goal: str = "",
74	        sub_goal_criteria: list[str] | None = None,
75	        unified_perception: str = "",
76	        available_elements: list[dict] | None = None,
77	        action_history: list[str] | None = None,
78	        last_error: str = "",
79	        page_type: str = "",
80	        action_type_hint: str = "",
81	    ) -> PlannedAction:
82	        """Plan the single next browser action.
83	
84	        Automatically chooses cold-start or warm-start mode based on
85	        whether enough scored outcomes exist in LanceDB.
86	        """
87	        # Determine mode
88	        record_count = self._intelligence.total_records
89	        use_memory = record_count >= _WARM_START_MIN_RECORDS
90	
91	        if use_memory:
92	            logger.debug("plan_next_action: warm start (%d records)", record_count)
93	            return await self._warm_start_plan(
94	                goal_context=goal_context,
95	                sub_goal=sub_goal,
96	                sub_goal_criteria=sub_goal_criteria,
97	                unified_perception=unified_perception,
98	                available_elements=available_elements,
99	                action_history=action_history,
100	                last_error=last_error,
101	                page_type=page_type,
102	                action_type_hint=action_type_hint,
103	            )
104	        else:
105	            logger.debug("plan_next_action: cold start (%d records)", record_count)
106	            return await self._cold_start_plan(
107	                goal_context=goal_context,
108	                sub_goal=sub_goal,
109	                sub_goal_criteria=sub_goal_criteria,
110	                unified_perception=unified_perception,
111	                available_elements=available_elements,
112	                action_history=action_history,
113	                last_error=last_error,
114	            )
115	
116	    # ── Cold start: LLM reasons from context ──────────────────
117	
118	    async def _cold_start_plan(
119	        self,
120	        goal_context: str,
121	        sub_goal: str = "",
122	        sub_goal_criteria: list[str] | None = None,
123	        unified_perception: str = "",
124	        available_elements: list[dict] | None = None,
125	        action_history: list[str] | None = None,
126	        last_error: str = "",
127	    ) -> PlannedAction:
128	        """Reason purely from context — no memory available yet.
129	
130	        The LLM receives: goal, sub-goal, page state, available elements,
131	        action history, and last error. It reasons step-by-step about what
132	        happened, why, and what should happen next.
133	        """
134	        user_prompt = build_planner_user_prompt(
135	            goal_context=goal_context,
136	            sub_goal=sub_goal,
137	            sub_goal_criteria=sub_goal_criteria,
138	            unified_perception=unified_perception,
139	            available_elements=available_elements,
140	            action_history=action_history,
141	            memory_recommendations=None,  # cold start — no memory
142	            last_error=last_error,
143	        )
144	
145	        try:
146	            response = await get_llm_client().complete_structured(
147	                system_prompt=PLANNER_SYSTEM,
148	                user_prompt=user_prompt,
149	                json_schema=PLANNER_JSON_SCHEMA,
150	                model_override=get_config().llm.planner_model,
151	            )
152	        except Exception:
153	            logger.warning("planner: LLM cold-start plan failed")
154	            return PlannedAction(
155	                action_type="wait",
156	                tool="wait",
157	                reasoning="LLM call failed during cold-start planning. Waiting for retry.",
158	                confidence=0.1,
159	                source="fallback",
160	            )
161	
162	        if response.parsed is None:
163	            logger.warning("planner: LLM returned unparseable cold-start plan")
164	            return PlannedAction(
165	                action_type="wait",
166	                tool="wait",
167	                reasoning=f"LLM returned unparseable response: {response.content[:200]}",
168	                confidence=0.1,
169	                source="fallback",
170	            )
171	
172	        return PlannedAction(
173	            action_type=response.parsed.get("action_type", "wait"),
174	            selector=response.parsed.get("selector", ""),
175	            value=response.parsed.get("value", ""),
176	            tool=response.parsed.get("tool", "wait"),
177	            reasoning=response.parsed.get("reasoning", ""),
178	            confidence=response.parsed.get("confidence", 0.5),
179	            expected_outcome=response.parsed.get("expected_outcome", ""),
180	            source="llm_cold",
181	        )
182	
183	    # ── Warm start: memory recommendations + LLM validation ───
184	
185	    async def _warm_start_plan(
186	        self,
187	        goal_context: str,
188	        sub_goal: str = "",
189	        sub_goal_criteria: list[str] | None = None,
190	        unified_perception: str = "",
191	        available_elements: list[dict] | None = None,
192	        action_history: list[str] | None = None,
193	        last_error: str = "",
194	        page_type: str = "",
195	        action_type_hint: str = "",
196	    ) -> PlannedAction:
197	        """Query memory for best past actions, then validate with LLM.
198	
199	        1. Build context text from current state
200	        2. Embed and query LanceDB for top-k similar successful actions
201	        3. Format recommendations for the LLM
202	        4. LLM validates: do recommendations make sense for CURRENT page?
203	        5. LLM outputs final action (may override recommendations)
204	        """
205	        embedder = get_embedding_generator()
206	        context_text = embedder.build_context_text(
207	            action_type=action_type_hint,
208	            selector="",
209	            tool="",
210	            goal_description=goal_context,
211	            page_type=page_type,
212	        )
213	
214	        # Query memory
215	        scored_actions = await self._intelligence.query_best_actions(
216	            action_type=action_type_hint,
217	            goal_description=goal_context,
218	            page_type=page_type,
219	            k=5,
220	            min_score=_MIN_RECOMMENDATION_SCORE,
221	        )
222	
223	        # Format recommendations for LLM
224	        memory_recommendations = _format_recommendations(scored_actions)
225	
226	        user_prompt = build_planner_user_prompt(
227	            goal_context=goal_context,
228	            sub_goal=sub_goal,
229	            sub_goal_criteria=sub_goal_criteria,
230	            unified_perception=unified_perception,
231	            available_elements=available_elements,
232	            action_history=action_history,
233	            memory_recommendations=memory_recommendations if memory_recommendations else None,
234	            last_error=last_error,
235	        )
236	
237	        try:
238	            response = await get_llm_client().complete_structured(
239	                system_prompt=PLANNER_SYSTEM,
240	                user_prompt=user_prompt,
241	                json_schema=PLANNER_JSON_SCHEMA,
242	                model_override=get_config().llm.planner_model,
243	            )
244	        except Exception:
245	            logger.warning("planner: LLM warm-start plan failed, using top recommendation")
246	            return _best_recommendation(scored_actions) or PlannedAction(
247	                action_type="wait",
248	                tool="wait",
249	                reasoning="LLM warm-start call failed. Falling back to wait.",
250	                confidence=0.1,
251	                source="fallback",
252	            )
253	
254	        if response.parsed is None:
255	            logger.warning("planner: LLM returned unparseable warm-start plan, using top recommendation")
256	            return _best_recommendation(scored_actions) or PlannedAction(
257	                action_type="wait",
258	                tool="wait",
259	                reasoning="LLM warm-start returned unparseable. Falling back to wait.",
260	                confidence=0.1,
261	                source="fallback",
262	            )
263	
264	        # Determine source: did LLM follow a recommendation or override?
265	        source = _classify_plan_source(response.parsed, scored_actions)
266	
267	        return PlannedAction(
268	            action_type=response.parsed.get("action_type", "wait"),
269	            selector=response.parsed.get("selector", ""),
270	            value=response.parsed.get("value", ""),
271	            tool=response.parsed.get("tool", "wait"),
272	            reasoning=response.parsed.get("reasoning", ""),
273	            confidence=response.parsed.get("confidence", 0.5),
274	            expected_outcome=response.parsed.get("expected_outcome", ""),
275	            source=source,
276	        )
277	
278	
279	# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
280	
281	
282	def _format_recommendations(scored_actions: list[ScoredAction]) -> list[dict]:
283	    """Format ScoredActions into a compact list for the LLM prompt."""
284	    if not scored_actions:
285	        return []
286	
287	    recs = []
288	    for i, sa in enumerate(scored_actions[:5]):
289	        recs.append({
290	            "rank": i + 1,
291	            "action_type": sa.action_type,
292	            "selector": sa.selector,
293	            "tool": sa.tool,
294	            "composite_score": round(sa.composite_score, 3),
295	            "outcome_score": round(sa.outcome_score, 3),
296	            "error_penalty": round(sa.error_penalty, 3),
297	            "use_count": sa.use_count,
298	            "similarity": round(1.0 - sa.distance, 3) if sa.distance else 0.0,
299	        })
300	    return recs
301	
302	
303	def _best_recommendation(scored_actions: list[ScoredAction]) -> PlannedAction | None:
304	    """Return the top recommendation as a PlannedAction, skipping fallback."""
305	    if not scored_actions:
306	        return None
307	    best = scored_actions[0]
308	    if best.composite_score < _MIN_RECOMMENDATION_SCORE:
309	        return None
310	    return PlannedAction(
311	        action_type=best.action_type,
312	        selector=best.selector,
313	        tool=best.tool,
314	        reasoning=(
315	            f"Top memory recommendation (score={best.composite_score:.2f}, "
316	            f"used {best.use_count}x). LLM validation skipped."
317	        ),
318	        confidence=best.composite_score,
319	        source="memory_validated",
320	    )
321	
322	
323	def _classify_plan_source(
324	    parsed: dict,
325	    scored_actions: list[ScoredAction],
326	) -> str:
327	    """Determine whether the LLM followed a recommendation or overrode it."""
328	    action_type = parsed.get("action_type", "")
329	    selector = parsed.get("selector", "")
330	
331	    for sa in scored_actions:
332	        if sa.action_type == action_type and sa.selector == selector:
333	            return "memory_validated"
334	
335	    if scored_actions:
336	        return "memory_override"  # LLM chose something different
337	    return "llm_cold"

8	/// The abstract browser interface.
9	///
10	/// Every browser engine (Chromium CDP, Servo, Firefox, `WebKit`) implements
11	/// this trait. The rest of the system never touches a concrete backend —
12	/// it only calls methods on `dyn BrowserBackend`.
13	///
14	/// # Safety
15	///
16	/// Implementations must guarantee that calling [`close`](Self::close) (or
17	/// dropping the backend) terminates the underlying browser process.
18	/// Zombie processes are a hard bug.
19	#[async_trait]
20	pub trait BrowserBackend: Send + Sync {
21	    /// Navigate to a URL. Returns page metadata after load completes.
22	    async fn navigate(&self, url: &str, timeout: Duration) -> Result<PageState, BrowserError>;
23	
24	    /// Get the current page's DOM as a distilled tree.
25	    async fn get_distilled_dom(&self, mode: DistillMode) -> Result<DistilledPage, BrowserError>;
26	
27	    /// Capture a screenshot. Returns raw PNG or JPEG bytes.
28	    async fn capture_screenshot(
29	        &self,
30	        format: ImageFormat,
31	        full_page: bool,
32	    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, BrowserError>;
33	
34	    /// Click an element by CSS selector.
35	    async fn click(&self, selector: &str) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;
36	
37	    /// Type text into an element identified by CSS selector.
38	    async fn type_text(&self, selector: &str, text: &str) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;
39	
40	    /// Select an option from a `<select>` element.
41	    async fn select_option(
42	        &self,
43	        selector: &str,
44	        value: &str,
45	    ) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;
46

50	        pixels: Option<i32>,
51	        selector: Option<&str>,
52	    ) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;

54	    /// Wait for the page to reach a stable loaded state.

I now have a complete picture of the browser launch pipeline. Here is the full report.

---

## Browser Launch Pipeline - Complete Analysis

### Architecture Overview

The system is split into two layers:

1. **Rust daemon** (`C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb`) -- owns Chromium lifecycle and CDP communication
2. **Python intelligence layer** (`C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\nerves`) -- connects to the Rust daemon via gRPC

---

### 1. Where Chromium Is Launched

**Primary launch location**: `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-cdp\src\process.rs`, function `ChromiumProcess::launch()` (line 41).

The launch chain is:

- `ans-daemon/src/main.rs` line 37-38: CLI parses `--prewarm` (default 2, 0 to disable)
- `main.rs` line 71-72: passes pool size to `IpcServer::new().with_pool_size(cli.prewarm)`
- `server.rs` line 79: `self.sessions = SessionManager::with_pool_size(size)` sets pool size
- `session.rs` line 74: `create()` calls `self.pool.acquire()`
- `pool.rs` line 69: `acquire()` calls `CdpBackend::launch(Some(StealthConfig::standard()))`
- `pool.rs` lines 117-118: The background `warmer_task` also calls `CdpBackend::launch(Some(StealthConfig::standard()))`
- `client.rs` line 149: `CdpBackend::launch()` calls `ChromiumProcess::launch(stealth.as_ref())`
- `process.rs` line 41: `ChromiumProcess::launch()` actually spawns the Chromium executable

---

### 2. Full Browser Launch Flags and Arguments

In `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-cdp\src\process.rs` lines 58-81, these base flags are always applied:

```
--headless=new
--remote-debugging-port=<ephemeral-port>
--user-data-dir=<temp-dir>/ans-chromium-<uuid>
--no-sandbox
--disable-gpu
--disable-dev-shm-usage
--disable-extensions
--disable-background-networking
--disable-sync
--disable-translate
--disable-default-apps
--hide-scrollbars
--metrics-recording-only
--mute-audio
--no-first-run
--window-size=1280,720
about:blank
```

Then, stealth flags are appended from `StealthConfig::chrome_flags()` based on the stealth level.

**Stealth flags** in `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-stealth\src\flags.rs`:

At **all levels** (even Off):
```
--disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled
--disable-features=ChromeWhatsNewUI,TranslateUI,...(many features disabled)
--disable-field-trial-config
--disable-hang-monitor
--disable-prompt-on-repost
--disable-component-update
--disable-domain-reliability
--disable-ipc-flooding-protection
--no-pings
--no-service-autorun
```

At **Standard** level and above:
```
--disable-web-security
--disable-features=SignedHTTPExchange,IsolateOrigins,site-per-process
--enable-features=NetworkService,NetworkServiceInProcess
--disable-client-side-phishing-detection
--disable-component-extensions-with-background-pages
--disable-crash-reporter
--disable-breakpad
--enable-automation
```

At **Aggressive** level and above:
```
--disable-features=VizDisplayCompositor
--disable-logging
--disable-background-timer-throttling
--disable-renderer-backgrounding
--disable-backgrounding-occluded-windows
--disable-popup-blocking
--disable-password-generation
--disable-save-password-bubble
--disable-infobars
--force-color-profile=srgb
--use-gl=swiftshader
--use-angle=swiftshader
```

At **Paranoid** level:
```
--disable-features=AudioServiceOutOfProcess
--disable-breakpad
--disable-crashpad
--disable-dev-shm-usage
--disable-gpu-sandbox
--disable-software-rasterizer
--enable-webgl
--enable-gpu-rasterization
--ignore-gpu-blocklist
--use-fake-device-for-media-stream
--use-fake-ui-for-media-stream
--disable-notifications
--disable-geolocation
--disable-reading-from-canvas
```

---

### 3. User-Agent and Webdriver-Related Code

**The `navigator.webdriver` patch**: `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-stealth\src\patches.rs`, function `patch_navigator_webdriver()` (line 103). Overrides `navigator.webdriver` to return `false`.

**The `navigator.platform` and `navigator.userAgent` patch**: `patches.rs` function `patch_navigator_platform()` (line 671). Sets `navigator.platform` to `"Win32"` (from the profile) and re-defines `navigator.userAgent` getter. Note: the userAgent getter currently returns `navigator.userAgent` unchanged (it's a self-reference that just re-exposes the browser's own UA string).

**User-Agent is NOT explicitly overridden**. The `patch_navigator_platform` function at line 688-691 sets up a getter for `navigator.userAgent` that simply returns the default browser value -- it does not substitute a custom user-agent string.

**Fingerprint profiles** are in `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-stealth\src\profiles.rs`. The default profile is `windows_chrome()` which defines:
- Platform: `"Win32"`
- WebGL: `"Google Inc. (Intel)"` / `"ANGLE (Intel, Intel(R) UHD Graphics ...)"`
- Screen: 1920x1080, 24-bit color
- Languages: `["en-US", "en"]`
- Timezone: `"America/New_York"`

**CDP-based injection mechanism**: Anti-detection scripts are injected via `Page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument` in `client.rs` lines 171-189, right after CDP domain enablement. This runs before any page script executes.

**Self-audit script**: `patches.rs` function `audit_script()` (line 69) generates JavaScript that checks whether `navigator.webdriver`, `window.chrome`, `navigator.plugins`, `navigator.languages`, `navigator.hardwareConcurrency`, `navigator.deviceMemory`, and `window.outerWidth` are all properly spoofed.

---

### 4. Chromium Executable Discovery

`process.rs` function `find_or_download_chromium()` (line 237) checks in order:

1. `CHROMIUM_OVERRIDE` environment variable
2. Playwright cache (`%LOCALAPPDATA%\ms-playwright` on Windows, `~/.cache/ms-playwright` on Linux, `~/Library/Caches/ms-playwright` on macOS)
3. PATH (searches for `chromium.exe`/`chrome.exe` on Windows, `chromium`/`chromium-browser`/`chrome`/`google-chrome` on Unix)
4. macOS bundles (`/Applications/Chromium.app/...`, `/Applications/Google Chrome.app/...`)
5. Windows install dirs (`LOCALAPPDATA\Chromium\Application\`, `ProgramFiles\Google\Chrome\Application\`, etc.)
6. Auto-download via `npx playwright install chromium`

---

### 5. CDP Connection Lifecycle

`C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-cdp\src\client.rs`:

- `CdpConnection::connect()` (line 41) opens a WebSocket to the page-level CDP URL
- Uses `tokio-tungstenite` for WebSocket communication
- A writer task pumps JSON-RPC commands from an mpsc channel to the WebSocket
- A reader task reads responses and dispatches them to pending command futures via oneshot channels
- Responses use an atomic `NEXT_ID` counter for correlation

`ChromiumProcess::launch()` discovers the CDP URL by:
1. Polling `http://127.0.0.1:{port}/json/version` for the browser-level `webSocketDebuggerUrl` (up to 20 attempts, 250ms apart)
2. Polling `http://127.0.0.1:{port}/json` for the page-level `webSocketDebuggerUrl`

---

### 6. Browser Pool and Pre-warming

`C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-ipc\src\pool.rs`:

- Default pool size from daemon CLI: `--prewarm 2`
- Configurable via `ans.toml` under `[chromium]`: `pool_size = 4`, `max_sessions_per_instance = 1`
- Background `warmer_task()` runs every 500ms, maintains `min_idle = max_size / 2` (minimum 1)
- On `acquire()`: pops from pool if available; otherwise cold-launches
- On `release()`: returns to pool if under capacity; otherwise closes the backend
- Python config also has `prewarm_chrome: bool = True` at `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\nerves\ans_nerves\config.py` line 65 (though this appears to be unused in the current codebase -- the prewarming is handled entirely in Rust)

---

### 7. CDP Command Module Structure

`C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-cdp\src\command.rs` implements typed CDP command builders:

- **Page domain**: `page_enable()`, `page_navigate(url)`, `page_get_frame_tree()`, `page_capture_screenshot(format, quality)`, `page_add_script_to_evaluate_on_new_document(source)`
- **DOM domain**: `dom_enable()`, `dom_get_document(depth)`, `dom_query_selector(node_id, selector)`, `dom_get_outer_html(node_id)`, `dom_get_box_model(node_id)`, `dom_scroll_into_view(node_id)`
- **Runtime domain**: `runtime_enable()`, `runtime_evaluate(expression)`
- **Input domain**: `input_dispatch_mouse_event(type, x, y, button)`, `input_dispatch_key_event(type, key)`, `input_insert_text(text)`
- **Browser domain**: `browser_get_version()`
- Response parsers: `parse_navigation_result()`, `parse_dom_node_id()`, `parse_query_selector_result()`, `parse_outer_html()`, `parse_box_model()`, `parse_evaluate_result()`, `parse_screenshot_result()`, `parse_frame_tree()`, `parse_page_info()`

---

### 8. Humanized Input

`C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-stealth\src\humanize.rs`:

- `bezier_path()`: Generates cubic bezier mouse movement paths with sub-pixel jitter
- `typing_delay()`: Variable delays per character (common letters 40-80ms, rare 60-120ms, uppercase 70-140ms)
- `think_delay()`: 200-800ms between actions
- `click_linger()`: 150-400ms linger after clicking
- `position_jitter()`: +/- 2px random offset from element center

Humanized behavior is gated by `StealthConfig::humanize` (default `true`) and applied in `client.rs` `click()` (line 373) and `type_text()` (line 443).

---

### 9. Relevant File Paths Summary

| Purpose | File |
|---------|------|
| Chromium launch (flags, discovery, download) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-cdp\src\process.rs` |
| CDP WebSocket client (connect, send, backend trait impl) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-cdp\src\client.rs` |
| CDP command builders and response parsers | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-cdp\src\command.rs` |
| ans-cdp lib (re-exports) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-cdp\src\lib.rs` |
| Stealth config (levels, adaptive escalation) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-stealth\src\lib.rs` |
| Chrome flags per stealth level | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-stealth\src\flags.rs` |
| CDP JS patches (webdriver, chrome, plugins, etc.) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-stealth\src\patches.rs` |
| Humanized input (bezier, typing delays) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-stealth\src\humanize.rs` |
| Fingerprint profiles (Windows/Mac/Linux, random variation) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-stealth\src\profiles.rs` |
| Browser pool (pre-warmed instances, warmer task) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-ipc\src\pool.rs` |
| Session manager (create/close/sessions) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-ipc\src\session.rs` |
| gRPC server implementation | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-ipc\src\server.rs` |
| Daemon entry point (CLI, prewarm flag) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-daemon\src\main.rs` |
| BrowserBackend trait (abstract browser interface) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-core\src\backend.rs` |
| Session/Action types | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\crates\ans-core\src\session.rs` |
| Proto definition (gRPC service) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\proto\ans.proto` |
| ans.toml config (chromium, pool, gateway) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\ans.toml` |
| Python gRPC client | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\nerves\ans_nerves\grpc_client.py` |
| Python config (prewarm_chrome flag) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\nerves\ans_nerves\config.py` |
| Python agent loop (creates browser session via gRPC) | `C:\Users\kusta\ILabs\visionweb\nerves\ans_nerves\planner\loop.py` |

---

### Key Gaps/Observations

1. **No explicit user-agent override**: The stealth layer redefines `navigator.platform` and sets up a getter for `navigator.userAgent`, but the getter just returns the browser default rather than a custom UA string. The `--user-agent` flag is never passed to Chromium.
